posts.thread_title: Does a Declining magnetic field prevent DHA from doing it’s “thing” in the brain?
posts.author: Mat.I
posts.content: I know this is ask jack and it is a good question. I was thinking about this when Jack mentioned the SAA issue. I would say that transport and spin state would definitely be impacted. To cross the BBB it is reliant on Mfsd2a (transporter) which is dependent on the sodium gradient (e.g. Na+/K+ atp pase etc). So you need a strong neg charge / redox pot.. when pump stalls the transporter looses it driving force = DHA in blood but unable to cross BBB etc.. what happens for people who have deuterated water tables? they will be far from this and dielec constant will be tanked to bulk state.. Re: spin issues - I would say this would drive chaotic spin states with unstable electrons that would oxidise the DHA before they can even be used effectively.. The CISS filter would be failing big time.. For you to make this a bit better in a "bad" location would be to consume higher amounts of DHA via only (no exceptions) wild caught cold water seafood and use CT (~ 4 deg C) to force expansion of EZ water layers.. Shit environments = baseline burn-rate of DHA would be climbing. It would be a loosing battle in the long term due to the amount of tax you are paying each day to bridge the gap which would be determined by your baseline mito het rate etc...

posts.author: Mr.AJ
posts.content: Walk around the outside and see where it is coming from. Normally one side is the strongest, then look for towers. If no towers on that side close by good chance it is the neighbors WiFi. In single story buildings shielding the fence with wire mesh (metal window screen) can be a quick easy fix.

posts.author: Jacks
posts.content: I got a similar dark spot (tear?) that you mention in my left eye last fall after driving up solo to Oregon for a week - though it moves around with the fluid in the eye like the floaters. It has not gone away but has become less bothersome as my brain appears to have adjusted to it and does not register it some of the time. I also have floaters that have been around for quite a while - again not always noticeable and depends on lighting conditions. Other than that I have no other vision issues except presbyopia which may be age-related (60) and I also blame intense blue-light abuse (ALAN) about 10-15 years ago. By the way, DH of a friend got a similar tear recently too. So this could be an early warning sign of the times! I have been doing facial ice-water dunks for a month now and see no significant change yet, but then again I am H1 haploytype and based on what Jack wrote in DM # 63 blog on eyes, I'm more susceptible to damage, and CT for photorepair is less effective for H1 compared to certain other haplotypes - though my eyes are brown so have more melanin than both my brothers. However, I am continuing every other day now with the CT but started DDW saline drops in my eyes first thing before I go out (just started a day or so ago). I do a lot of grounding and sun throughout the day but my environment sucks, so not expecting miracles till I move. I eat plenty of seafood and nuts and always use blueblockers when on laptop. I think being in ES will heal this issue, but jus…

posts.thread_title: WHY YOU MUST BE DISCERNING WITH AI
posts.author: Johan Lindøm
posts.content: If you had asked an AI before you hit ENTER on your first two attempts, it would have told you that you sounded like a brick. AI can help us with so many things — diplomacy, dialogue, and much more. The real problem is that there’s a serious need for better communication skills, and I know this is true for me. I’m dyslexic, and these tools have literally saved me by letting me express myself properly. Spellcheck in the ’80s was a game-changer. My kids use tools like Lindy’s, and it’s amazing how much it helps them. I often come across as a brick even though I don’t mean to — I simply never had proper spelling or writing training. Writing everything the wrong way didn’t improve anything. Getting the message written is already hard enough. By the time I try to phrase things in a decent, polite, and thoughtful way, I’m completely out of energy. I learned to be polite at home, but combining that with fighting the words and sentences at the same time is really tough. I’m often perceived as a “brick” or an insensitive person. That’s why getting corrections is so useful. I’ve noticed my skills are improving even without AI now. It’s a real game-changer. Even reading blogs on paper has improved. These improvements also come from the techniques and hacks I’m learning from Doctor Jack. My brain works better.

I've been with him since I was 19 and I feel like these new routines have set off a whole new "honey-moon"-phase much stronger and better than the first one. This is very interesting to me because that is probably how relationships and marriages are meant to feel, when we are grounded and connected to one another. We've struggled A LOT through the years. This feels like winning. I am so happy about that Updates on my routine I have been using the TENS-unit on my ear and vibration which is incredible. posts.author: Pejzman
posts.content: Dr. Kruse, What’s your take on astrology and its different branches — for example, astrocartography? I’ve heard that ancient civilizations like the Maya and Egyptians used astronomical and celestial knowledge for major decisions, such as timing wars, construction projects, and leadership choices. Maya rulers, for example, aligned important events with Venus cycles. Furthermore, I’ve also heard claims that some modern elites use this kind of information for timing and strategic advantage. Do you see all forms of astrology as pure pseudoscience, or do you think there may be something real to it? I’d appreciate hearing your thoughts. Thanks.
